programme.title: Head Hunters

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, grabbing three items she hopes to transform, sell on and send off to new homes. Reclamation duo the Junk Gypsies are tasked with turning a pair of teak legs into something stunning, while furniture experts the Three Restorers take on a broken desk. The trio of eclectic tip-finds is rounded off with the item Sarah will work on herself - a formica kitchen table and three chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper start their antiques hunt in the New Forest. Raj dives into the history of an outdoor swimming, while Margie discovers an incredible art collection gifted to the people of Bournemouth. The duo then head west to Exeter for auction(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany and Keegan face a race against the clock as they head to Scotland to tie the knot. With Karen, Mitch, Sonia and Jack in hot pursuit, will the teenage couple make it down the aisle? Back in the Square, as Lisa gets increasingly annoyed at Mel taking over with Louise, Mel celebrates Ruby buying the E20. Jay decides the time has come to step back from the undertaker's, while Ben seizes the opportunity to help Callum earn more money(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Adil Ray assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Surprises are in store when Fleabag attends her godmother's exhibition of erotic art, having managed to acquire a date for the occasion. She finds herself confronted with a declaration of love and a very familiar piece of work, but Claire is notable by her absence. The awkward artistic gathering forces Fleabag to face up to the consequences of her actions - as well as finally revealing what happened to her best friend Boo. Conclusion of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the eighth live episode of this year's series, as the remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will follow in the footsteps of the latest eliminated duo, Emma Weymouth and Aljaz Skorjanec(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, Jared and Sophie, a couple from Essex, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance from the Royal Albert Hall, in the presence of HM the Queen and members of the royal family. Throughout the festival, artists including James Blunt, Leona Lewis and Jeff Goldblum will perform alongside the Central Band of the Royal Air Force and the Band of HM Royal Marines(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Arsenal at the King Power Stadium and Chelsea v Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge. The Foxes won last season's corresponding fixture 3-0, with second-half goals from Youri Tielemans and a Jamie Vardy brace securing all three points for Brendan Rodgers' side. Plus, action from Burnley v West Ham United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Bournemouth at St James' Park, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Friday night's fixture between Norwich City and Watford at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Oakland Raiders v Los Angeles Chargers at RingCentral Coliseum(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Arsenal at the King Power Stadium and Chelsea v Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge. The Foxes won last season's corresponding fixture 3-0, with second-half goals from Youri Tielemans and a Jamie Vardy brace securing all three points for Brendan Rodgers' side. Plus, action from Burnley v West Ham United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Bournemouth at St James' Park, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Friday night's fixture between Norwich City and Watford at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall, with the Queen watching proceedings from the balcony of the Foreign and Commonwealth Office as the Prince of Wales lays a wreath on behalf of the nation. On Horse Guards Parade, Sophie Raworth hears stories of courage and sacrifice from those taking part(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items of value at the Three Counties Showground in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire. Presented by Eric Knowles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the National Memorial Arboretum near Lichfield in Staffordshire to see how its tranquil surroundings bring comfort and hope to those who have lost loved ones in the armed forces. Sean Fletcher chats to Chelsea Pensioner and Britain's Got Talent Winner Colin Thackery, who performs a special arrangement of the inspirational song You Raise Me Up(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents more updates, scores and goals from today's first-round fixtures(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Highlights of the programme in which Kate Humble and Ben Fogle report from behind the scenes at Longleat Safari Park in Wiltshire, featuring the vets trying to save a binturong with a mystery illness, and Jean Johansson meets the baby meerkats(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is with the Essex Wildlife Trust on Fingringhoe Wick - a newly created stretch of saltmarsh - as he helps the team do its first-ever winter fish survey, Margherita Taylor hears about a project to preserve the spirit of the Dig for Victory campaign, before joining Adrian Thomas from the RSPB to learn about the organisation's new sound archive. Adam Henson meets a farmer feeding his cows charcoal, and Tom Heap asks if enough is being done in rural areas to support veterans(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by West End star Luke Evans and a group dance marking Remembrance Sunday(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra leaves the familiar surroundings of Jordan College and Oxford behind as she and Pantalaimon settle into their new life in London, but she is determined to try to find Roger and hopes that Mrs Coulter will be able to help in her mission. Meanwhile, Farder Coram and the Gyptians continue their search for the missing children and the elusive and mysterious Gobblers. Jack Thorne's adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: On a Polish mission as part of the SOE, Harry grasps a second chance at saving Kasia from the horrors of Warsaw, while new mother Lois is surprised to be visited again by Vernon. Robina continues to appreciate her unlikely and surprising bond with Douglas and Jan, while Nancy promises Webster she will do all she can to help Albert(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including the top-of-the-table clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield. The Citizens pipped Liverpool to the title by one point last season, but it was the Reds who had set the pace so far this term. Plus, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa, Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste reveal how scammers were able to con one woman out of her money not once, but twice(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: An extended edition sees Monty Don add autumn perennials to his borders, show how to take root cuttings and reveal plans for next year when he plants bulbs for spring colour. On her Kent allotment, Frances Tophill catches up on some seasonal sowing and invites a special guest to help her with a small building project. Nick Bailey visits Cambridge University Botanic Garden, where he finds out how and why some plants produce their scent at the end of the day. The programme also returns to the wildlife garden of Kate Bradbury to find out about the lives of solitary bees(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Nick Robinson and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Bryn Williams, Kim Woodward, Ryan Simpson and Atul Kochhar pair up with their chosen home cook partners, with Pierre Koffmann on hand to judge their final dishes(n)

programme.title: Street Auction
programme.desc: Paul Martin helps raise funds for a deserving member of a local community, using hidden treasures found in their street(n)

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A visit to the Bala Autumn Sale in Snowdonia, north Wales, which is one of the UK's most important sheep dog auctions. Narrated by Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Warwick Davis starts his research with his paternal grandmother Edith, who led to his big break at the age of 11 when she heard a radio advert. That advert was 'looking for short people to appear in Return of the Jedi', and Warwick was catapulted to stardom, continuing his acting career in the likes of Labyrinth and Willow. He takes a non-judgemental approach as he researches the family line stretching back from Edith, finding humanity and humour in some uncomfortable stories, while on his maternal side, he finds out about a postman who lived a double life(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin visits another location, inviting members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the couples' progress as they prepare for tomorrow night's show. Plus, Gethin Jones reports from the studio rehearsals and interviews with celebrity fans and there's a live music performance in the It Takes Two studio(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Johnny Ball, Suzi Ruffell, Nina Wadia and Danny Wallace go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Dulwich Hamlet v Carlisle United (Kick-off 7.55pm). Coverage of the first-round tie at Champion Hill. The Hamlet defeated fellow National League South side Havant & Waterlooville in the final qualifying round to reach this stage of the competition for the first time since 1998 and will now look to produce an upset against a Carlisle side that have been struggling for form in League Two. Dan Walker presents, with analysis from Micah Richards and Karen Carney(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Olga Koch, Nigel Ng and Suzi Ruffell and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the music programme. Jools and Jamie discuss jazz legend Miles Davis' recently released album Rubberband, recorded in 1985. Jamie also delves into the archive to pick his favourite performance from the show's history and shares some of his current favourite new artists as well as performing a track from his recent album, Taller. Electro-pop band Metronomy, rapper Kano and jazz collective Nerija also perform, while rising south London star Joy Crookes makes her Later debut performing new single Early, joined by star of the burgeoning Dublin hip-hop scene Jafaris(n)

programme.title: Kings of Curry (2013)
programme.desc: Two brothers who are both chefs fall out, and decide to split their family recipe book in two, opening competing restaurants on the same street. After 20 years of rivalry, the daughter of one of the pair is determined to reconcile her father and uncle so they can both attend her wedding. Comedy, starring Amara Karan and Harish Patel(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The crime documentary reveals how a 1970s miscarriage of justice, involving three teenage boys, led to a new method of police interrogation and investigation. When the body of Maxwell Confait was found in south London, three local boys confessed to arson and murder and were consequently convicted, despite retracting their statements and protesting their innocence. However, each of their convictions were eventually overturned, which shone a light on dangerous police practices that pressured the boys to confess to a crime they did not commit(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby League
programme.desc: New Zealand v Great Britain (Kick-off 7.00am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the third Test in a four-match tour for the Lions, which takes place at Christchurch Stadium. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Featuring a group of dogs riding Russia's underground rail system, and a clever crow that can use a pedestrian crossing to crack a tough problem(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: For animals growing up is a real adventure, but finding food, avoiding predators and making friends doesn't always come naturally, so they need a little parental guidance to help them learn and hone the skills that will enable them to survive on their own. David Tennant narrates this documentary following the trials and tribulations of young animals all over the world as they prepare to leave home(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles between homemade fighting robots, including a towering, sword-wielding warrior built by a Mensa member and his three sons, that is one of the tallest machines to ever enter the arena and runs on wheels that are the height of a washing machine. Other contestants include a mechanical lobster and a 19-year-old self-taught engineer's creation, which has teeth that spin at 9000RPM. The conflict takes a dramatic turn when one of the robots bursts into flames(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Brian McFadden and Keith Duffy are on the hunt for antiques in the dales of Derbyshire, as well as assisting a mountain rescue team and visiting a traditional clockmaker(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace hosts the first week of the culinary contest, which sees the first six professional chefs competing for a place in this week's quarter-final. Half of the competitors are challenged by Marcus Wareing to prepare poached guinea fowl breast with a Sauce Robert, while Monica Galetti sets the rest the challenge of making hand-cut tagliatelle with a lamb ragu. Finally, they must prepare a signature dish to decide who will stay on and who will be eliminated(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs are challenged to recreate Marcus' crab omelette Arnold Bennett, before the second trio take on Monica's puff pastry and vanilla mousseline layered dessert. Moving into the second round, the six chefs are then tasked to impress the judges with a signature dish of their own design(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An insight into the life of one of Hollywood's best-loved actresses, telling the story of her Oscar-winning career by using rarely seen archive footage of her appearances on the BBC(n)

programme.title: Jane Eyre (1943)
programme.desc: A naive young woman becomes governess in a mysterious Yorkshire mansion and before long she is completely besotted with her charismatic but intimidating employer. But unbeknown to her, the master of the house is concealing a dark secret. Romantic period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Joan Fontaine, Orson Welles, Agnes Moorehead and Margaret O'Brien(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: England v Germany (Kick-off 5.30pm). All the action from the friendly, played in front of a capacity crowd at Wembley Stadium. The match is set to break the record attendance for a women's football match in Britain, set during the 2012 Olympics, and England will be looking to treat the fans to a much-improved performance after struggling for form in their post-World Cup matches so far. The last meeting between the sides came in last year's SheBelieves Cup in New York and ended in a 2-2 draw, and another closely fought encounter is anticipated(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and the platoon dress as firemen to take part in an initiative exercise, but soon find themselves tackling an all-too-real blaze with only Victorian equipment to rely on. Vintage comedy, starring Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and John Le Mesurier. First broadcast in 1972(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on an adventure aboard the transcontinental Indian Pacific Railway, from Adelaide to Perth. Along the way he hears the story of an enterprising 19th-century Briton with a murky past who made his money growing vines, strikes it lucky in one of the most lucrative goldfields in the world and pays a flying visit to the doctor to hear how patients in remote Western Australia are cared for. He also hears how Edward VIII was caught up in a train accident near Bridgetown and learns about a child migration programme that cast a dark shadow on the history of both the UK and Australia(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig 'quarrels', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Jason Manford, Aisling Bea and Anuvab Pal at the brunt of her angst. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: Documentary examining the impact of novels from three perspectives - the empire, class experience and, in this first film, women's voices. The programme aims to show the plight of women is a theme that reaches right back to the earliest novels, with extracts from Jane Austen, the Brontes, Mary Shelley and Virginia Woolf, through to the post-war publishing boom where a new generation of global writers such as Zadie Smith, Toni Morrison, Arundhati Roy and Margaret Atwood have continued to speak out for women to a new generation of readers(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: Following their split, and with Ricky having joined the House of Wintour, Damon and Ricky compete against each other when they are called to audition for a spot on Madonna's Blonde Ambition tour. American drama, starring Ryan Jamaal Swain and Dyllon Burnside(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: Pray Tell is in the middle of organising the annual Aids cabaret at the hospital when his own health takes a sudden turn for the worst. As he fights for his life, a few unexpected visitors show up at his hospital bedside. Blanca is left to oversee the event, but suffers from feelings of guilt as she at the same time she tries to save her business premises from being closed down. US drama, starring Billy Porter and MJ Rodriguez(n)

programme.title: Testament of Youth (2014)
programme.desc: When her fiance and brother are sent to the front line during the early days of the First World War, a young woman gives up her studies at Oxford University to follow them. She becomes a nurse, and the horrors she witnesses during the conflict change her outlook on life forever. Period drama based on the memoirs of writer Vera Brittain, starring Alicia Vikander, Kit Harington and Taron Egerton(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Great Chelsea Garden Challenge
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants head to Painswick Rococo Garden in the heart of the Cotswolds, where they have to create conceptual gardens that showcase their creativity and impress the judges. James Alexander-Sinclair and Ann-Marie Powell then decide which three go forward to the final. Joe Swift presents(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don heads to India, where he takes in some of the most masterful examples of horticultural and architectural extravagance in the world - including the gardens of the Taj Mahal. Along the way, the green-fingered presenter investigates the majestic tomb gardens of the Mughal emperors, and a unique rock sculpture garden created from discarded objects and rubbish(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team looks at all things autumnal, with Ellie Harrison on Alderney where autumn storms have washed up seaweed that is providing a seasonal feast for an unexpected animal. John Craven takes a walk in the footsteps of John Keats to explore the landscape that inspired his famous poem To Autumn, while Anita Rani is on the farm of Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks to get some hints on pickling and preserving autumn produce(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's nearest neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the first quarter-final, inventing a dish with rice as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - William Sitwell, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents updates, scores and goals from today's first-round fixtures(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Novak Djokovic v Matteo Berrettini. Coverage of day one from The O2 in London, where the opening singles round-robin match takes place. The season-ending tournament will see the top eight available singles players in the world compete for this prestigious title, which was won last year by Alexander Zverev when he defeated Djokovic in the final(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the weekend's first-round ties, including Dover Athletic v Southend United at Crabble Athletic Ground, Barnet v Fleetwood Town at The Hive Stadium and Chippenham Town v Northampton Town at Hardenhuish Park, as the non-league teams looked to produce upsets against their Football League opponents. With analysis from Kevin Kilbane and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: Coverage of this morning's commemorations from the Cenotaph in Whitehall, as the Queen, members of the Royal Family and the Prime Minister gathered for a two-minute silence in memory of the service personnel who gave their lives in both World Wars and later conflicts(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: After three months at sea, the test pilots from the Royal Navy and RAF have successfully landed the F35 jets on the flight deck of HMS Queen Elizabeth for the first time, and even tested launching and landing in hurricane conditions. But with just a few weeks to go before the warship arrives in New York in spectacular style, they are about to try a method of landing never attempted before, which carries great risks but could revolutionise carrier operations globally. Narrated by Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the world's first carbon neutral country by 2021(n)

programme.title: They Shall Not Grow Old (2018)
programme.desc: Peter Jackson's documentary exploring the First World War, edited from rarely seen archive footage from the Western Front which has been restored and colourised, while lip readings of the men featured in the footage have allowed their speech to be reconstructed. The film also includes archive interviews with servicemen from the BBC and the Imperial War Museum(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents the concluding action from the second round of the season at the Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome in Glasgow. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Richard Skinner present the edition first aired on October 6, featuring performances by the Pasadenas, Bobby McFerrin, the Wee Papa Girl Rappers, Spear of Destiny, the Beatmasters with PP Arnold, Kim Wilde, Erasure, Rick Astley, U2 and Duran Duran(n)

programme.title: It's Only Rock 'n' Roll: Rock 'n' Roll at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of classic rock 'n' roll artists and songs in celebration of the musical genre, Including performances by Jerry Lee Lewis, Dion, Dick Dale and Neil Sedaka. Plus, a selection of songs with rock 'n' roll in the title, such as Tom Petty's Anything That's Rock 'n' Roll, Joan Jett's I love Rock 'n' Roll and Oasis's Rock 'n' Roll Star(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Gary Davies present the edition first aired on October 13, featuring performances by the Beatmasters with PP Arnold, Sabrina, T'Pau, Enya, The Christians, D Mob featuring Gary Haisman, Kim Wilde, Sinitta, Whitney Houston and Bananarama(n)

programme.title: The Beatles: Made on Merseyside
programme.desc: Few dispute that the Beatles defined 1960s music and popular culture like no other band, but how John, Paul, George and Ringo made the journey from Merseyside teenagers to international pop stars is less known. Director Alan Byron's documentary recounts the rise of the band as American rock 'n' roll and rhythm and blues dragged post-war Liverpool into one of the most vibrant music cities ever with the Mersey Beat sound(n)

programme.title: Forever Young - How Rock 'n' Roll Grew Up
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the ways rock 'n' roll stars have coped with growing old after being symbols of rebellion from the genre's roots in the 1950s. The programme uncovers how artists have enjoyed comebacks in the 21st century, and examines what happened to the mantra 'live fast, die young'. Featuring contributions by Iggy Pop, Lemmy, Rick Wakeman, Suggs and Alison Moyet. Narrated by Cherie Lunghi(n)

programme.title: Tom Jones at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the singer's performances, from the start of his career in the 1960s to his appearance on Later with Jools Holland just before he celebrated his 70th birthday in 2010. The programme includes archive footage of the Welshman's appearances on The Dusty Springfield Show and Top of the Pops(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Richard Skinner present the edition first aired on October 6, featuring performances by the Pasadenas, Bobby McFerrin, the Wee Papa Girl Rappers, Spear of Destiny, the Beatmasters with PP Arnold, Kim Wilde, Erasure, Rick Astley, U2 and Duran Duran(n)

programme.title: It's Only Rock 'n' Roll: Rock 'n' Roll at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of classic rock 'n' roll artists and songs in celebration of the musical genre, Including performances by Jerry Lee Lewis, Dion, Dick Dale and Neil Sedaka. Plus, a selection of songs with rock 'n' roll in the title, such as Tom Petty's Anything That's Rock 'n' Roll, Joan Jett's I love Rock 'n' Roll and Oasis's Rock 'n' Roll Star(n)

programme.title: The Beatles: Made on Merseyside
programme.desc: Few dispute that the Beatles defined 1960s music and popular culture like no other band, but how John, Paul, George and Ringo made the journey from Merseyside teenagers to international pop stars is less known. Director Alan Byron's documentary recounts the rise of the band as American rock 'n' roll and rhythm and blues dragged post-war Liverpool into one of the most vibrant music cities ever with the Mersey Beat sound(n)

programme.title: Nature's Wonderlands: Islands of Evolution
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Richard Fortey travels to Madeira in the north Atlantic to examine what happens to a volcanic island chain as it nears the end of its life cycle and starts sinking back into the sea. He explores the remains of an ancient forest that once carpeted all of Europe, encounters lizards that live to be four times older than their mainland counterparts, and discovers an unexpectedly rich marine habitat populated by whales, dolphins and unusual deep-sea species(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: With his role in the investigation irreparably tarnished, Roban loses his grip on the case, while Laure and Gilou decide to reactivate the now-dormant money-laundering operation. In French(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Tintin is tasked with investigating Laure and Gilou's increasingly unscrupulous - and illegal - methods, while Josephine does her best to protect an uncooperative Lola. In French(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder applies to join the Royal Flying Corps, seeing it as a great way of getting out of the trenches and into a classroom. Comedy, guest starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson, with Rowan Atkinson and Tony Robinson(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder sees a chance to get some rest while hunting for a spy in the field hospital, and decides he is not going to let his holiday be cut short just because he has found an obvious suspect with a German accent. Comedy, guest starring Miranda Richardson, with Rowan Atkinson and Tony Robinson(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder tries every trick in the book to escape the trenches before the big push, including pretending to be insane - but ends up going over the top with sidekicks Baldrick and George. Final episode of the comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Palmer, with Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Mark Goodier present the edition first shown on September 29, featuring performances by Sinitta, U2, Hazell Dean, Alexander O'Neal, Duran Duran, Erasure, Whitney Houston, Bananarama, the Hollies and T'Pau(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988

programme.title: Nature's Wonderlands: Islands of Evolution
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Richard Fortey travels to Madeira in the north Atlantic to examine what happens to a volcanic island chain as it nears the end of its life cycle and starts sinking back into the sea. He explores the remains of an ancient forest that once carpeted all of Europe, encounters lizards that live to be four times older than their mainland counterparts, and discovers an unexpectedly rich marine habitat populated by whales, dolphins and unusual deep-sea species(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species(n)

programme.title: Chalkie Davies: Rock Photographer
programme.desc: Documentary following the preparations for the former New Musical Express photographer's first major exhibition at the National Museum of Wales in Cardiff. Chalkie, who became a favourite among punk and new wave bands such as the Clash, the Specials and Squeeze, looks back on an extraordinary life behind the camera, while old friends including Elvis Costello reflect on how his images have proved so enduring(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents action from the second round of the season at the Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome in Glasgow. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank's Monuments of Remembrance
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories behind First World War cemeteries and memorials, including those at the Somme and Gallipoli, as well as those responsible for creating them(n)

programme.title: Everything Is Connected - George Eliot's Life
programme.desc: In this Arena programme, contemporary artist Gillian Wearing celebrates the legacy of Victorian novelist George Eliot. Just as Eliot's novel Middlemarch explored the lives of ordinary men and women, this experimental film is made up of a diverse cast of people from different backgrounds, and features Jason Isaacs and Sheila Atim as the narrators(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The Rosetta mission to comet 67P was the first time a spacecraft landed on a comet's surface. Cameras reveal what this icy body taught us about the dawn of the solar system and the origins of life on Earth(n)

programme.title: Palace for the People

programme.title: Great War Horses
programme.desc: An account of the men and horses of the Australian Light Horse regiments, and the pivotal role they played in the First World War at the Battle of Romani in 1916, the Light Horse charge at the Battle of Beersheba in 1917, and the capture of Damascus in 1918. Bred for Australia's tough Outback conditions, the horses, known as Walers, were well equipped for the harsh climate and terrain of the Middle East, where the Anzac forces faced the armies of the Ottoman Empire(n)

programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, grabbing three items she hopes to transform, sell on and send off to new homes. Reclamation duo the Junk Gypsies are tasked with turning a pair of teak legs into something stunning, while furniture experts the Three Restorers take on a broken desk. The trio of eclectic tip-finds is rounded off with the item Sarah will work on herself - a formica kitchen table and three chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper start their antiques hunt in the New Forest. Raj dives into the history of an outdoor swimming, while Margie discovers an incredible art collection gifted to the people of Bournemouth. The duo then head west to Exeter for auction(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany and Keegan face a race against the clock as they head to Scotland to tie the knot. With Karen, Mitch, Sonia and Jack in hot pursuit, will the teenage couple make it down the aisle? Back in the Square, as Lisa gets increasingly annoyed at Mel taking over with Louise, Mel celebrates Ruby buying the E20. Jay decides the time has come to step back from the undertaker's, while Ben seizes the opportunity to help Callum earn more money(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Adil Ray assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Wow! Wales promotes genealogy tourism and all of the staff do DNA tests to find out how Welsh they are, while the 'King of Wales' pays a visit from America. Comedy, starring Sally Phillips, with guest appearances by Scott Quinnell and Stuart Milligan(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Shelley, a businesswoman who was a finalist in 2010's Miss Wales, has not gone out in public with no make-up for years - but now faces the challenge of five dates without it. As always, stylist Nathan is on hand to help her build up her confidence and offers her his own personal twist on the classic little black dress(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Surprises are in store when Fleabag attends her godmother's exhibition of erotic art, having managed to acquire a date for the occasion. She finds herself confronted with a declaration of love and a very familiar piece of work, but Claire is notable by her absence. The awkward artistic gathering forces Fleabag to face up to the consequences of her actions - as well as finally revealing what happened to her best friend Boo. Conclusion of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Tom Brown and Anna Haugh, and special guest Sir Trevor McDonald. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: The Lord Mayor's Show
programme.desc: Coverage of the annual ceremonial parade through London(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including tomorrow's Premier League clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: New Zealand v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents action from the third Test in a four-match series for the Lions, which took place at Christchurch Stadium(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents action from the opening events in the second round of the season at the Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome in Glasgow, including the team pursuit team sprint and a number of par-cycling events. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts as Happy Mondays frontman Shaun Ryder, actress Arabella Weir, Paralympian Stef Reid and Countryfile presenter Sean Fletcher take to the black chair to answer questions on their specialist subjects the history of Manchester, Motown girl groups, the parables of Jesus, and Sesame Street, before being tested on their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a writers special edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Laurence Marks, Maurice Gran, Terry Deary, Eve Pollard, Jojo Moyes, Jeffery Deaver, Peter James and Dreda Say Mitchell(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the eighth live episode of this year's series, as the remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will follow in the footsteps of the latest eliminated duo, Emma Weymouth and Aljaz Skorjanec(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, Jared and Sophie, a couple from Essex, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance from the Royal Albert Hall, in the presence of HM the Queen and members of the royal family. Throughout the festival, artists including James Blunt, Leona Lewis and Jeff Goldblum will perform alongside the Central Band of the Royal Air Force and the Band of HM Royal Marines(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Arsenal at the King Power Stadium and Chelsea v Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge. The Foxes won last season's corresponding fixture 3-0, with second-half goals from Youri Tielemans and a Jamie Vardy brace securing all three points for Brendan Rodgers' side. Plus, action from Burnley v West Ham United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Bournemouth at St James' Park, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Friday night's fixture between Norwich City and Watford at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Oakland Raiders v Los Angeles Chargers at RingCentral Coliseum(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall, with the Queen watching proceedings from the balcony of the Foreign and Commonwealth Office as the Prince of Wales lays a wreath on behalf of the nation. On Horse Guards Parade, Sophie Raworth hears stories of courage and sacrifice from those taking part(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items of value at the Three Counties Showground in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire. Presented by Eric Knowles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the National Memorial Arboretum near Lichfield in Staffordshire to see how its tranquil surroundings bring comfort and hope to those who have lost loved ones in the armed forces. Sean Fletcher chats to Chelsea Pensioner and Britain's Got Talent Winner Colin Thackery, who performs a special arrangement of the inspirational song You Raise Me Up(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents more updates, scores and goals from today's first-round fixtures(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of Sainsbury's. After a challenging Christmas, the staff go all out to maximise profits with their flower sales over the Mother's Day weekend. Plans are also made to sell a birthday cake to celebrate the company's 150th year of trading, bringing in Claire Ptak, the creator of Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding cake, for ideas. Plus, last year CEO Mike Coupe announced a possible merger with Asda. Will industry regulators give the company the go-ahead to become the biggest supermarket in the country?(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is with the Essex Wildlife Trust on Fingringhoe Wick - a newly created stretch of saltmarsh - as he helps the team do its first-ever winter fish survey, Margherita Taylor hears about a project to preserve the spirit of the Dig for Victory campaign, before joining Adrian Thomas from the RSPB to learn about the organisation's new sound archive. Adam Henson meets a farmer feeding his cows charcoal, and Tom Heap asks if enough is being done in rural areas to support veterans(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by West End star Luke Evans and a group dance marking Remembrance Sunday(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra leaves the familiar surroundings of Jordan College and Oxford behind as she and Pantalaimon settle into their new life in London, but she is determined to try to find Roger and hopes that Mrs Coulter will be able to help in her mission. Meanwhile, Farder Coram and the Gyptians continue their search for the missing children and the elusive and mysterious Gobblers. Jack Thorne's adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: On a Polish mission as part of the SOE, Harry grasps a second chance at saving Kasia from the horrors of Warsaw, while new mother Lois is surprised to be visited again by Vernon. Robina continues to appreciate her unlikely and surprising bond with Douglas and Jan, while Nancy promises Webster she will do all she can to help Albert(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including the top-of-the-table clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield. The Citizens pipped Liverpool to the title by one point last season, but it was the Reds who had set the pace so far this term. Plus, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa, Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate how technology enables people to be caught out by elaborate online romance fraud(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A railway worker's £300,000 personal injury claim for a broken thumb hits the buffers when undercover filming proves he is taking his insurers for a ride, and a fraudulent claim for a mobile phone lost in a river sinks like a stone when the handset resurfaces in a recycling shop. Ore Oduba presents(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: An elderly man's home is broken into and expensive watches of great sentimental value are taken. Thankfully, a neighbour's security cameras video the thief in the act. Plus, a prolific burglar targets businesses across a whole area, including a pizza shop, a pub and a chippy. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma is furious to discover Heston's memorial bench has been stolen and resolves to track down the thief. Elsewhere, Al and Jimmi re-launch the Icon, but have their efforts paid off? Sid tries to help a desperate father who wants more access to his estranged son and is prepared to break the law to make it happen(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a sports lecturer, a customer adviser and a businessman must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Highlights of the programme in which Kate Humble and Ben Fogle report from behind the scenes at Longleat Safari Park in Wiltshire, featuring the vets trying to save a binturong with a mystery illness, and Jean Johansson meets the baby meerkats(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is with the Essex Wildlife Trust on Fingringhoe Wick - a newly created stretch of saltmarsh - as he helps the team do its first-ever winter fish survey, Margherita Taylor hears about a project to preserve the spirit of the Dig for Victory campaign, before joining Adrian Thomas from the RSPB to learn about the organisation's new sound archive. Adam Henson meets a farmer feeding his cows charcoal, and Tom Heap asks if enough is being done in rural areas to support veterans(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by West End star Luke Evans and a group dance marking Remembrance Sunday(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra leaves the familiar surroundings of Jordan College and Oxford behind as she and Pantalaimon settle into their new life in London, but she is determined to try to find Roger and hopes that Mrs Coulter will be able to help in her mission. Meanwhile, Farder Coram and the Gyptians continue their search for the missing children and the elusive and mysterious Gobblers. Jack Thorne's adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: On a Polish mission as part of the SOE, Harry grasps a second chance at saving Kasia from the horrors of Warsaw, while new mother Lois is surprised to be visited again by Vernon. Robina continues to appreciate her unlikely and surprising bond with Douglas and Jan, while Nancy promises Webster she will do all she can to help Albert(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including the top-of-the-table clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield. The Citizens pipped Liverpool to the title by one point last season, but it was the Reds who had set the pace so far this term. Plus, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa, Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures, including Celtic v Motherwell at Celtic Park and Livingston v Rangers at Tony Macaroni Arena(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate how technology enables people to be caught out by elaborate online romance fraud(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A railway worker's £300,000 personal injury claim for a broken thumb hits the buffers when undercover filming proves he is taking his insurers for a ride, and a fraudulent claim for a mobile phone lost in a river sinks like a stone when the handset resurfaces in a recycling shop. Ore Oduba presents(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: An elderly man's home is broken into and expensive watches of great sentimental value are taken. Thankfully, a neighbour's security cameras video the thief in the act. Plus, a prolific burglar targets businesses across a whole area, including a pizza shop, a pub and a chippy. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma is furious to discover Heston's memorial bench has been stolen and resolves to track down the thief. Elsewhere, Al and Jimmi re-launch the Icon, but have their efforts paid off? Sid tries to help a desperate father who wants more access to his estranged son and is prepared to break the law to make it happen(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a sports lecturer, a customer adviser and a businessman must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton headsa to Pembrokeshire to help a couple with a £325,000 budget find a suitable rural home for their retirement. While in Wales, Alistair also visits an upland farm to discover the challenges and advantages of rearing native south Wales mountain sheep(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, grabbing three items she hopes to transform, sell on and send off to new homes. Reclamation duo the Junk Gypsies are tasked with turning a pair of teak legs into something stunning, while furniture experts the Three Restorers take on a broken desk. The trio of eclectic tip-finds is rounded off with the item Sarah will work on herself - a formica kitchen table and three chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper start their antiques hunt in the New Forest. Raj dives into the history of an outdoor swimming, while Margie discovers an incredible art collection gifted to the people of Bournemouth. The duo then head west to Exeter for auction(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany and Keegan face a race against the clock as they head to Scotland to tie the knot. With Karen, Mitch, Sonia and Jack in hot pursuit, will the teenage couple make it down the aisle? Back in the Square, as Lisa gets increasingly annoyed at Mel taking over with Louise, Mel celebrates Ruby buying the E20. Jay decides the time has come to step back from the undertaker's, while Ben seizes the opportunity to help Callum earn more money(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Adil Ray assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Scottish comedian Ashley Storrie joins regular jokesters Neil Delamere, Jake O'Kane, Colin Murphy and Tim McGarry to poke fun at the people and events which have been making the news at home and abroad over the past week(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Surprises are in store when Fleabag attends her godmother's exhibition of erotic art, having managed to acquire a date for the occasion. She finds herself confronted with a declaration of love and a very familiar piece of work, but Claire is notable by her absence. The awkward artistic gathering forces Fleabag to face up to the consequences of her actions - as well as finally revealing what happened to her best friend Boo. Conclusion of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Tom Brown and Anna Haugh, and special guest Sir Trevor McDonald. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: The Lord Mayor's Show
programme.desc: Coverage of the annual ceremonial parade through London(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including tomorrow's Premier League clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: New Zealand v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents action from the third Test in a four-match series for the Lions, which took place at Christchurch Stadium(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents action from the opening events in the second round of the season at the Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome in Glasgow, including the team pursuit team sprint and a number of par-cycling events. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts as Happy Mondays frontman Shaun Ryder, actress Arabella Weir, Paralympian Stef Reid and Countryfile presenter Sean Fletcher take to the black chair to answer questions on their specialist subjects the history of Manchester, Motown girl groups, the parables of Jesus, and Sesame Street, before being tested on their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a writers special edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Laurence Marks, Maurice Gran, Terry Deary, Eve Pollard, Jojo Moyes, Jeffery Deaver, Peter James and Dreda Say Mitchell(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the eighth live episode of this year's series, as the remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will follow in the footsteps of the latest eliminated duo, Emma Weymouth and Aljaz Skorjanec(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, Jared and Sophie, a couple from Essex, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance from the Royal Albert Hall, in the presence of HM the Queen and members of the royal family. Throughout the festival, artists including James Blunt, Leona Lewis and Jeff Goldblum will perform alongside the Central Band of the Royal Air Force and the Band of HM Royal Marines(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Arsenal at the King Power Stadium and Chelsea v Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge. The Foxes won last season's corresponding fixture 3-0, with second-half goals from Youri Tielemans and a Jamie Vardy brace securing all three points for Brendan Rodgers' side. Plus, action from Burnley v West Ham United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Bournemouth at St James' Park, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Friday night's fixture between Norwich City and Watford at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Oakland Raiders v Los Angeles Chargers at RingCentral Coliseum(n)

programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall, with the Queen watching proceedings from the balcony of the Foreign and Commonwealth Office as the Prince of Wales lays a wreath on behalf of the nation. programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Over 30 years on, the scale and magnitude of the Piper Alpha disaster continues to shock. Iona Ballantyne's father Bob was a well-known spokesman for survivors and their families, and a delayed response to grief was felt strongly by Iona years after his death. To understand and process her own thoughts and loss she visits other families, who like her, were direct witnesses to the consequences of the disaster and the changes it forced upon them(n)

programme.title: Tenable
