programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Sophie, Alex, Laura and Ed pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vanessa is suspicious of Charity, who soon finds herself with some explaining to do, but eventually she and Vanessa are hopeful for their future(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ali tells Gary that he's sure Maria will be interested to see that he's been operating as a loan shark. Robert is horrified to discover Tyler and Amy have become mates, and Geoffrey ensures that Yasmeen sees him looking at a dating site, saying it is research for his radio show(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh is forced to question his loyalty to his own partner when Cole is investigated by the FBI. Meanwhile, Cole works a case that puts Natalie's fiance Andrew in the crosshairs, right before their wedding day. Final episode of the action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: A model, a gardener, a cage fighter and Mr Luton are the latest candidates to enter the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Jasmine from Suffolk will not settle for romance unless it's amazing. She wants a man with a passion for life and with plenty of interests - and he absolutely must love animals. In an attempt to find Mr Right, she looks round the homes of three potential partners before choosing one for a date - but will any give a clue to the kind of man she is after?(n)

programme.title: Meet the Parents
programme.desc: Dating show, hosted by Holly Willoughby, which puts the love lives of singletons in the hands of the people they know best - their mum and dad. Each week, one man and one woman must choose from three potential partners, with their decisions solely based on an encounter with the contestants' parents(n)

All would be well if he'd only not built the caravan out of Mrs. Wicket's garden she(n)

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: The arrival of spring sees the crowds return to Snowdon, with railwayman Steff re-opening the summit cafe and singer Shirley Bassey unveiling a train carriage named in her honour. The return of paying customers is good news for Alwena and her cafe 1,800ft up the mountain, but the busy paths to the summit spell trouble for Rob and his mountain rescue team(n)

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK

Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking three contestants to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a Good Morning Britain special of the quiz show, as Piers Morgan, Susanna Reid, Richard Arnold and Kate Garraway pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Mountain-biking champion Tom, plus-sized model and author Peter, Royal Marine Joel and musician Caolan are the latest candidates to enter the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the next stage in the star-studded version of the singing contest, which features further live performances in London. They will have the support and coaching of the judges, with Simon Cowell taking charge of the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger mentoring the Overs, and Louis Walsh looking after the Unders(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh is forced to question his loyalty to his own partner when Cole is investigated by the FBI. programme.title: Bang on the Money
programme.desc: Rickie Haywood-Williams and Melvin Odoom host the game show in which two teams of friends must battle it out to hit a specific target number 'bang on' in a series of physical and mental challenges. programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner demonstrate a selection of one-pot dishes. John starts with pork and chorizo stew, followed by his one-pot rice. Lisa makes coq au vin blanc, finishing with apple and blackberry cobbler for dessert(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha joins forces with Mandy Yin, the guru of Malaysian cuisine, to create a family feast from Malaysia. The menu comprises Kaya French toast for breakfast, king prawn laksa for lunch, Nadia's chicken curry for dinner and Mandy's ble pandan pancakes for dessert(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the next stage in the star-studded version of the singing contest, which features further live performances in London. The remaining acts have got through the initial rounds in Los Angeles and the judges' deliberation, and now must battle it out for the chance to be crowned champion and win a record deal. They will have the support and coaching of the judges, with Simon Cowell taking charge of the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger mentoring the Overs, and Louis Walsh looking after the Unders(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Bachelor Cal goes looking for love by checking out the houses of three potential dates in the North West - and this former professional footballer appears to have a huge choice when it comes to picking one to take out on a date. There is a pop music lover, a fitness fanatic and the 'Beyonce of the caravan park'. But which one will he want to score a date with?(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince (2009)
programme.desc: The young wizard is given the task of uncovering the secrets of Lord Voldemort's past as the evil sorcerer's henchmen cause havoc in both the magical and Muggle worlds. With the help of headmaster Dumbledore, Harry ventures into the memories of those who knew the Dark Lord during his early years, hoping to gain an insight into the secret behind his apparent immortality. However, Voldemort's influence extends much closer to home than expected, as Harry's rival Malfoy is chosen to carry out a sinister mission. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Michael Gambon, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Tom Felton and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly Come Dancing professional Oti Mabuse, tennis coach Judy Murray and actor-comedian John Thomson to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly may think they know who they are, but in this two-part documentary, the popular presenters uncover their surprising and emotional family histories, using a combination of DNA and genealogy. The shock revelations include military men in both of their families - one a medal-winning war hero, the other a deserter. And as well as delving into the past, Ant and Dec also get to meet DNA relatives they never knew existed(n)

programme.title: Charles: 50 Years a Prince
programme.desc: Fifty years ago in Caernarfon at his investiture, Prince Charles embarked on a unique relationship with a country that has shaped his life and work. While acknowledging a sometimes troubled and turbulent history, this documentary celebrates Wales through the eyes of a prince who has made the role his own. Narrated by Luke Evans(n)

programme.title: Fifty Shades of Green with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: To celebrate half a century spent in gardens around the world, the broadcaster visits his favourite horticultural locations around the UK with friends and experts who share his passions. He meets food writer Mary Berry at his favourite vegetable plot, goes inside Prince Charles' garden at Highgrove, takes a tour of Griff Rhys Jones' epic space and and strokes the hallowed turf on Wimbledon's Centre Court. Alan also returns to the allotment where, as a toddler, he was first introduced to gardening(n)

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Sophie, Alex, Laura and Ed pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vanessa is suspicious of Charity, who soon finds herself with some explaining to do, but eventually she and Vanessa are hopeful for their future(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ali tells Gary that he's sure Maria will be interested to see that he's been operating as a loan shark. Robert is horrified to discover Tyler and Amy have become mates, and Geoffrey ensures that Yasmeen sees him looking at a dating site, saying it is research for his radio show(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Jasmine from Suffolk will not settle for romance unless it's amazing. She wants a man with a passion for life and with plenty of interests - and he absolutely must love animals. In an attempt to find Mr Right, she looks round the homes of three potential partners before choosing one for a date - but will any give a clue to the kind of man she is after?(n)

programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the next stage in the star-studded version of the singing contest, which features further live performances in London. The remaining acts have got through the initial rounds in Los Angeles and the judge's deliberation, and now must battle it out for the chance to be crowned champion and win a record deal. They will have the support and coaching of the judges, with Simon Cowell taking charge of the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger mentoring the Overs, and Louis Walsh looking after the Unders(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Rib-tickling collection of camcorder calamities, narrated by Harry Hill(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (1991)
programme.desc: A bookish woman agrees to take her father's place as prisoner of a fearsome monster living in a castle deep in the forest. She gradually grows to see a gentler, more human side to her hideous host, and comes to realise she may be able to break the curse that turned him into a beast in the first place. Disney animated fantasy, with the voices of Paige O'Hara and Robby Benson(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (1991)

programme.title: Step Up 2: The Streets (2008)
programme.desc: A rebellious street dancer gets the chance to hone her skills at a prestigious school for the performing arts, but she struggles to fit into her new environment and is ostracised by her old friends. Instead, she sets about forming a new dance troupe with fellow students to compete in an underground competition. Drama, starring Briana Evigan, Robert Hoffman and Adam G Sevani(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Rock of Ages (2012)
programme.desc: An aspiring singer falls for a band's lead vocalist while working as a waitress in a live music club. The owner of the venue hopes to turn its struggling fortunes around by booking a famous rock act - but ends up faced with protests against the controversial star. Musical featuring rock songs from the 1980s, starring Julianne Hough, Tom Cruise, Diego Boneta, Catherine Zeta-Jones and Russell Brand(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Juice
programme.desc: Keith hosts the cheeky game show, with Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood, actor Phil Daniels, Loose Women presenter Ayda Field Williams, Love Island 2018 winner Dani Dyer, presenter Angela Scanlon and show favourite Joe Swash(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie questions everything he has ever won after receiving a participation medal at school, while Peter causes a fight between Lois's parents(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Charlie is stuck in France and unable to take Bet to the ball. Liz and Steve move out of the Queens and back to number 11. Kevin is stunned to discover his fine has been paid(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Charlie spends the night at the Rovers, Alma refuses to let Kevin pay her the money back, and Sally is upset that she will not see Jonathan again(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jay's question puts Rachel on the spot, and Chris turns Frank's offer down. At the Woolpack, Shirley is disguised to put a plot in motion(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Julie is shattered when her past catches up with her, Josh's invitation leaves Kathy on the hop, and the authors of Beckindale's scandal sheet are finally caught out(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny falls victim to conmen who have devised an elaborate loan and savings racket to fleece unwitting villagers. Meanwhile, Vernon, Bernie and David put their literary prowess to the test by entering a poetry competition run by the Ashfordly Gazette. Rural drama, starring Sarah Tansey and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey organises an exhibition as the bus company celebrates its half-centenary. Stan reminisces, and on falling asleep finds himself at the wheel of a bus from the turn of the 20th century. Comedy. starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is offered a job in a car factory in the midlands and decides the best way to quit his job at the bus depot is by getting himself sacked in order to earn an extra week's wages. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is Chris's first day in his new job, and Frank spots an old flame. At Emmerdale, it seems as though Sarah's having second thoughts(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah's plans go awry when three becomes a crowd. Disaster befalls the Windsors on their big day. At Home Farm, Frank is astounded by Jack's change of heart(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is annoyed when Mike refuses to talk about his cash flow problem. Sally wants Kevin to apologise to Joe. Maureen is upset when Reg does not invite Curly to the wedding(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma offers to remortgage the cafe for Mike but he refuses. Reg is upset to find no one has prepared a farewell party for him. Kevin confronts Sally about Joe(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Demolition man Charlie goes away to work, leaving his wife and children at home. Then, when the job is finished, he unwinds in a gay bar and is attacked by a homophobic thug - losing his wallet in the process. Not wanting police involvement, he goes home with no money, but eventually comes clean about a sexual encounter with a fellow worker and his world collapses around him. Vincent Regan and Julia Ford star, with Will Mellor(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Cath Hanley fears the worst when her son Ian is accused of killing a taxi driver - until she realises he is covering for the real culprit, his cousin Gary. However, her sister Jan refuses to let Gary hand himself in, leading to bitter conflict between two mothers desperate to keep their children out of jail. Gina McKee, Lorraine Ashbourne and Matt Smith star(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Hans Christian Andersen: My Life as a Fairy Tale
programme.desc: Part one of two. The rags-to-riches tale of the storyteller's life, giving an insight into the people who inspired his fantasy world. Drama, starring Kieran Bew, James Fox, Simon Callow, Emily Hamilton, Geraldine James and Alison Steadman(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: With Arthur having been gone for six months, Stan accompanies his sister Olive to the divorce courts. Reg Varney and Anna Karen star(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is having difficulty supporting the whole family on his meagre earnings. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Bullard asks Barnaby to investigate the disappearance of a friend's daughter who was a member of the Oblong Foundation, a cult based at Malham Hall. Jones goes under cover as a new recruit to solve the mystery, but he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion, which could be linked to the missing woman. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Will Knightley, Kevin Doyle, Holly Aird, Barbara Flynn and John Woodvine(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans directs this episode, set against the backdrop of the first moon landings in 1969. The detective investigates the deaths of an astrophysicist and his girlfriend in a tragic car accident as the rest of the world eagerly awaits news from Apollo 11. When he begins to suspect foul play, Endeavour enlists Thursday's help in getting to the truth, visiting the observatory where the dead man worked and discovering the possibility that he was not the intended victim. The case is complicated further when the dead scientist is revealed to have been part of a mysterious cult-like group(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The search for a serial killer in the area around Loch Ness becomes a matter of life and death for local detective Annie Redford, who is trying to cope with her first murder case. When the body of a local man is found at the foot of Carn Mohr Mountain and an isolated human heart on the loch shore, the town's normality is shattered and the nightmare begins. Thriller, starring Laura Fraser and Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Olive and Mum go on a spending spree, leaving Stan to foot the bill. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jenny's life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. Lewis and Hathaway discover he had been imprisoned for trying to kill well-known atheist Tom Rattan and had only recently been released. The inspector soon has reason to believe the Rattan family is hiding something from him - but nothing prepares him for the evidence Dr Hobson uncovers at the post-mortem. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star, with guest appearances by Jenny Seagrove and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars(n)

programme.title: The Theory of Everything (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of Stephen Hawking, exploring the renowned astrophysicist's romance with future wife Jane during their time at university in the 1960s and his initial diagnosis with motor neurone disease, which doctors believed would lead to his death within two years. Undaunted by deteriorating health, he continued his groundbreaking research into the origins of the universe. Starring an Oscar-winning Eddie Redmayne, with Felicity Jones and David Thewlis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Bullard asks Barnaby to investigate the disappearance of a friend's daughter who was a member of the Oblong Foundation, a cult based at Malham Hall. Jones goes under cover as a new recruit to solve the mystery, but he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion, which could be linked to the missing woman. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Will Knightley, Kevin Doyle, Holly Aird, Barbara Flynn and John Woodvine(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans directs this episode, set against the backdrop of the first moon landings in 1969. The detective investigates the deaths of an astrophysicist and his girlfriend in a tragic car accident as the rest of the world eagerly awaits news from Apollo 11. When he begins to suspect foul play, Endeavour enlists Thursday's help in getting to the truth, visiting the observatory where the dead man worked and discovering the possibility that he was not the intended victim. The case is complicated further when the dead scientist is revealed to have been part of a mysterious cult-like group(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The search for a serial killer in the area around Loch Ness becomes a matter of life and death for local detective Annie Redford, who is trying to cope with her first murder case. When the body of a local man is found at the foot of Carn Mohr Mountain and an isolated human heart on the loch shore, the town's normality is shattered and the nightmare begins. Thriller, starring Laura Fraser and Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Olive and Mum go on a spending spree, leaving Stan to foot the bill. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jenny's life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. Lewis and Hathaway discover he had been imprisoned for trying to kill well-known atheist Tom Rattan and had only recently been released. The inspector soon has reason to believe the Rattan family is hiding something from him - but nothing prepares him for the evidence Dr Hobson uncovers at the post-mortem. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star, with guest appearances by Jenny Seagrove and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars(n)

programme.title: Football's Greatest: Eusebio
programme.desc: A profile of the former Portuguese footballer, widely considered as one of the greatest strikers of all time having scored 473 goals for Benfica and 41 for his country(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A psychologist asks Theo to find her fiance, who has gone missing while on parole and may be planning to settle an old score. Paul Michael Glaser (Starsky and Hutch) guest stars(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The body of a man who died of internal bleeding mysteriously vanishes from the morgue, leading to accusations that Quincy may be up to no good(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's prestige as a pillar of the community is boosted by an invitation to address a meeting of his local Rotary Club - which is being held in the very hotel where Terry is acting as temporary house detective. However, a pair of thieves posing as priests and a mangeress equally as crooked as Arthur threaten to ruin his grand moment. Drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A missing small-time crook turns up dead, leading to a murder investigation - but Carter's girlfriend develops a peculiar interest in the proceedings. Geraldine James guest stars, with Dennis Waterman and John Thaw(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. Bodie and Doyle find themselves relentlessly pursued by a gang of assailants that seems able to accurately anticipate their every move, leading to a tense car chase and a riverside shoot-out. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar plays his part in a gangster's plot to trick his four greatest enemies into carrying out a robbery. Stephanie Beacham features in an early role, alongside Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo goes undercover as a chemist to catch a gang who stole a shipment of morphine from a pharmaceutical company - but the case is complicated when one of the crooks overdoses(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A kidnapped teenager is buried alive with less than 24 hours' supply of oxygen, and the only clue to help police solve the case is a half-eaten apple(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry intervenes in a tug-of-love dispute concerning an Anglo-Italian Mancini family, and finds himself unwittingly threatening the success of Arthur's latest money-making venture - a health club. Dennis Waterman and George Cole star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's former girlfriend visits from East Germany and he finds himself framed for carrying out an assassination attempt - leaving Doyle with no choice but to bring him in for questioning. Drama, starring Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A crook arranges the conversion of replica firearms into the real thing and sells them on to small-time hoodlums. When one of the arms misfires killing both a policeman and its holder, Regan is intent on discovering the source before more innocent lives are lost. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Champion of Champions Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening semi-final on day five from Ricoh Arena in Coventry, where the winners from group one and group four face each other over the best of 11 frames for a place in the final on Sunday. At last year's tournament, Ronnie O'Sullivan continued his march to the title by defeating Shaun Murphy 6-3 at this stage. Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Neal Foulds and Alan McManus, and commentary by Clive Everton, David Hendon and Phil Yates(n)

programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.desc: Brandon Adams takes on Levgen Khytrov, while Michael Moore almost gets into a brawl outside the ring. Meanwhile, one fighter faces a doctor evaluation(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents a look ahead to the day's racing at Wincanton and news on the final day of the flat season at Doncaster(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A youth is arrested after an elderly money-lender is brutally assaulted and robbed. His mother asks Regan, an old friend, to intervene. Though it is later proved he got a legitimate loan and is innocent of theft, the youngster finds himself being blackmailed after falling foul of some very dangerous individuals. Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Columbo: How to Dial a Murder (1978)
programme.desc: After discovering that his best friend and his wife were having an affair, a renowned psychologist uses his unique mind-control powers to commit a bizarre murder - with the help of two pet dogs conditioned to attack when they hear a certain command. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Nicol Williamson, Frank Aletter and Kim Cattrall(n)

programme.title: Nijinsky's Triple Crown
programme.desc: Short sports film about the legendary racehorse(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.35 Badger Ales Trophy Handicap Steeple Chase from Wincanton, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races from 1.30, 2.05 and 3.15 at Doncaster, and 2.40 at Aintree. With analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and Jason Weaver, reports by Matt Chapman, Oli Bell and Alice Plunkett, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: World of Sport: Petrol Heads
programme.desc: Richard Henwood looks back at archive motorsport action, featuring the likes of James Hunt and Barry Sheene, with a spectacular crash involving Kelvin Burt(n)

programme.title: The Way West (1967)
programme.desc: A senator and a veteran scout lead a band of settlers on an arduous journey across the great plains of 19th-century America. However, they face the prospect of reprisals from Native Americans when one of the pioneers accidentally shoots a child. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Robert Mitchum, Lola Albright, Michael Witney, Richard Widmark and Sally Field in her big-screen debut(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: An Audience with Billy Connolly
programme.desc: The comedian descends on London to entertain a celebrity crowd including Twiggy, Ringo Starr, Michael Brandon, Samantha Fox and Clive James, covering topics including ageing, parties and his idea for a new national anthem(n)

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling
programme.desc: Action ahead of the pay-per-view main event, held at Royal Farms Arena in Baltimore, Maryland(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry fall under the spell of a bewitching Australian beauty who has ventured from Down Under in search of her missing fiance. However, she may just happen to be concealing an ulterior motive. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. The hitherto innocent son manages to play-off his parent's ex-cohorts against one another with increasing dexterity and appears only too keen to pick up the duplicitous traits of his late father. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw(n)

programme.title: Fifth Gear
programme.desc: Adrian Simpson test-drives the Mercedes E Class, Tiff Needell compares the Subaru Impreza WRX STi with its rival the Mitsubishi Lancer Evolution 7 and Vicki Butler-Henderson tackles an off-road course for novice bikers inspired by the Paris-Dakar rally. Quentin Willson discovers that a Rolls-Royce Silver Shadow can be picked up for a mere £7,000(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. He disapproves of his daughter's villainous boyfriend, and Regan suspects he is being exploited to help break the romantic union. However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in(n)

programme.title: The Unremembered - Britain's Forgotten War Heroes
programme.desc: A century ago, in the aftermath of the First World War, Britain established vast war cemeteries to honour each individual who died serving the nation. The Commonwealth War Graves Commission set up to run them became famous for its principle of equality in death for everyone, regardless of the military rank or their role in civilian life. David Lammy journeys across Kenya and Tanzania to find out why many African soldiers and porters were overlooked during the conflict, and their names forgotten. He also looks at the impact this has on modern Africa and its communities today, and why he believes Britain has to put this right(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks with artist Ashley Jackson in Holmfirth and to the top of Wansfell Pike in the Lake District(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A glamorous globetrotter has her heart set on a retirement property in warmer climes and plans to relocate with her sister to South Africa's Garden Route. The only cloud on Cornwall-based Alma Scholes' horizon is having to leave behind her beloved Great Dane(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow experiences what New York was like at the height of the Industrial Revolution. Millions fled to escape the persecution, poverty and famine of 19th-century Europe, only to be confronted by living conditions worse than those they had left behind. He explores the overcrowded slums of Lower Manhattan, which were a breeding ground for cholera and typhoid, and has a go at cooking using clothes dye and floor cleaner - ingredients that were added to meals to disguise the smell of spoiled meat. He also discovers how certain feats of engineering transformed not only the city, but the world(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: Investigating one of the worst air disasters for two decades, Malaysia Airlines flight MH17 from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur, which crashed, killing 298 passengers on July 17, 2014. It occurred four months after the disappearance of Malaysian Airlines flight MH370, and has led to speculation that only a powerful ground-to-air missile could be responsible, but there are eyewitness accounts of other aircrafts seen flying next to MH17 close to impact. This documentary tracks down eye witnesses, and speaks with secret intelligence sources in an attempt to sort fact from fiction(n)

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: The series exploring Britain's criminal underworld returns, beginning with profiles of Liverpool-based gangsters Stephen 'the Devil' French and Boris Boszomenyi(n)

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham(n)

programme.title: Escort Girls (1974)
programme.desc: Seven people who are alone in London on Christmas eve turn to an escort agency for comfort. However, things do not work out as simply as planned, as they wrestle with their personal demons and endure traumatic experiences of violence, racism and crime. Drama, starring Maria O'Brien and David Dixon(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary investigating scientific breakthroughs in research on Alzheimer's disease, which are bringing hope to millions of sufferers across the world. New scanning and gene technology is allowing scientists to identify the disease at its earliest stages, often 15 years before symptoms appear and brain cells are destroyed, with drug trials in Colombia, USA and Europe showing startling success. The programme also reveals the changes in lifestyle that can prevent the development of the disease, as well as the effects of a UK-wide trial(n)

programme.title: The Wind of Change (1961)
programme.desc: An unemployed teenager in Notting Hill grows increasingly frustrated with his life, his anger manifesting as a deep-seated racism. programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks into the past of Edinburgh's Palace of Holyroodhouse, which is located close to the dormant volcano Arthur's Seat. Events in the royal residence, one of the smallest palaces in Britain, have determined the futures of England, France and Scotland, and it was the last place in which a royal prince challenged the right of an English king to sit on the throne. Fiona also examines objects including the Darnley jewel, which features numerous hidden messages, and the needlework of a doomed queen(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

Romantic drama, starring Danielle Panabaker and Matt Long(n)

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley and BBC 1Xtra DJ and part-time TV reviewer Sideman Allday find out how tech can help avoid overly pricey TV subscriptions. Georgie Barrat appraises Segway's latest personal transport kit, which 'follows' people around even when not being ridden. Ortis Deley heads for the hills to test out Google's latest mapping innovation, which allows anyone to record 'street view' themselves. Plus, the G Team goes back to university, hoping to teach a group of second-year students how to cook with three of the hottest kitchen gadgets around. Presented by Craig Charles(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A stunning journey in New Zealand, through the heart of South Island's best scenery. It starts in Greymouth, renowned for its coal mining past, moving on to Arthur's Pass National Park - with its historically important Maori hunting grounds. The train passes through Cass, made famous by Kiwi artist Rita Angus and onwards to the Waimakiriri gorge and its 15 tunnels and four viaducts. Rail engineers recall how the line has been affected by earthquakes before the journey arrives in Christchurch, a city sitting on a volcano. Narrated by Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: Caravanning with Shane Richie
programme.desc: The Isle of Wight is where Shane holidayed as a child, and where he began his career, staying in a unique, rock 'n' roll-themed caravan complete with its own hot tub. On his first day, Shane revisits his past with a visit to the pier at Sandown, where he did his earliest professional performances. Back at the holiday park, Shane enjoys an evening at the park's outdoor cinema, meeting both the holidaymakers who have come along and the staff keeping them entertained. He is later joined by TV chef Tony Tobin to forage along the shoreline for edible seaweed(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Dale Winton narrates a compilation of disastrous game show moments. Featuring contestants on The Million Pound Drop failing to pay attention to a question with a fortune at stake, the least successful player to appear on The Chase, the woman who broke her leg on The Krypton Factor and still finished third, Catchphrase host Nick Weir damaging his career by falling down the stairs, and the Princess Diana-themed episode of Channel 5 quiz show 100%(n)
